_comment: LISTENING AID: revision 3 with the synth part SILENCED, so the cloud can be judged alone. Revisions 1 and 2 fed the deck material rendered from Spotykach itself, which shares its oscillators, filters and grit, so the deck and the synth were hard to tell apart. This uses 96_Stormy_Noise_Loop_01.wav (48 kHz, 24-bit stereo): atonal, spectrally dense, no pitch of its own. That is the point -- the cloud is pitch-quantized and chord-locked, so it MAKES harmony out of unpitched material, and that trick is invisible when the source is already tonal. Timeline: record 1-9s, MOTION opens 10/16/22/28, STEP chop from 32s.

init._comment: part A silenced: zero base AND LEVEL modulation off -- the lane is active by boot default and will lift the part off a zero base on its own
part B (the room) at full LEVEL, MOTION parked and unmodulated so the sweep below is the only thing moving it

events._comment: 1-9s: record the storm live. The cloud should be audible and GROWING while this runs -- the fill-follows promise.
10-31s: MOTION opens the scatter from a tight loop to fog. The spec's acceptance criterion.
32s: the groove chops the texture into bursts.
